question_text: A concave mirror is used to focus sunlight. Explain why the light rays converge at the focal point.
correct_answer: The curved surface of the concave mirror reflects parallel light rays inward toward a single point (the focal point) because each ray hits the mirror at a different angle and reflects according to the law of reflection.
explanation: A concave mirror curves inward like a bowl. Each incoming ray reflects at the angle determined by its position on the curve, and these reflections converge at the focal point, concentrating the light's energy.
